presentation.title: Program Coach Deck 01 — Platform Integration
presentation.subtitle: How will I make my venture work in the real world?

slides.title: PLAN — Build Your Financial Enterprise
slides.body:
Week 4 Day 1 · Venture Blueprint Sprint

PLAN. | BUILD. | GROW. | LEAD.
slides.speakerNotes:
Scene 1 — Welcome to Week 4.
Reset the room. Introduce Blueprint Week.
Guiding question: How will I make my venture work in the real world?

slides.title: The Journey So Far
slides.body:
Week 1 — Discover: Dream Board, Your Why
Week 2 — Validate: Customer Persona, Customer Validation
Week 3 — Advise: FEC, Venture Proposition, Business Concept
Now — Plan: Build Your Financial Enterprise
slides.speakerNotes:
Scene 2 — Pride → Curiosity.
Walk the arc slowly: Discover → Validate → Advise → Plan.
Say: Look at how far you've already come.
You've laid the foundation. Now we connect it to a real platform.
discussionQuestions: Which artifact from Weeks 1–3 best represents your venture today?

slides.title: Your Venture Isn't Finished Yet
slides.body:
Dream ✓ · Customers ✓ · Solutions ✓ · Business Idea ✓ · FEC (Draft) ✓
────────────
Business Engine ❓

What's missing?
slides.speakerNotes:
Scene 3 — Pause after the checklist.
Ask: What's missing?
Do not answer immediately.
The missing piece is connecting the venture to an operating platform — not more ideas.
discussionQuestions: What's missing from your venture today?

slides.title: Our Mindset — Think Like an Entrepreneur. Build Like a Leader.
slides.speakerNotes:
Scene 4 — Set the Week 4 mindset before the business reveal.
Purpose: create value. Growth: build systems. Leadership: develop people. Legacy: build something that outlives you.

slides.title: What Business Are You Really Building?
slides.body:
If I gave you an AIA code today, what business would you be in?

Not insurance. Not just financial advisor.
You are building YOUR Financial Advisory Enterprise.
slides.speakerNotes:
"Insurance is what you deliver. Financial Advisor is the role you play. But the business you're building is an enterprise."
"Insurance is the industry. Financial Advisor is your profession. But your venture is a Financial Advisory Enterprise."
Poll the room before the reveal. Then connect: today we connect that enterprise to the AIA Platform.
discussionQuestions: If I gave you an AIA code today, what business would you be in?

slides.title: The AIA Platform
slides.body:
Products · Systems · Technology · Training · Compensation · Leadership

Built for You. Backed by AIA.
slides.speakerNotes:
Scene 6 — Walk the six pillars.
AIA is not replacing their business — it becomes the platform that powers it.
Products, systems, technology, training, compensation, leadership.
discussionQuestions: Which platform pillar will matter most for your venture?

slides.title: How Your Enterprise Generates Value
slides.body:
Personal Production → Renewals → Overrides → Leadership Income

Four stages. One enterprise. Infinite potential.
slides.speakerNotes:
Scene 7 — Walk the value flow.
Serve Clients → Personal Production → Renewals → Leadership → Enterprise.
Create impact. Earn income. Build legacy.
discussionQuestions: Which stage of the value flow is furthest from where you are today?

slides.title: Platform Integration
slides.body:
Your venture already exists. Today we connect it to a scalable business platform.

YOUR VENTURE → AIA PLATFORM → BUSINESS ENGINE → SCALABLE ENTERPRISE
slides.speakerNotes:
Scene 8 — Everything this morning leads here.
Over three weeks they already built their venture — they already have a business.
AIA provides products, systems, training, compensation, leadership.
AIA is not replacing their business — it powers it.
When venture connects to platform, it becomes an operating business with a business engine.
Now it can grow through systems, recurring income, leadership, and people they develop.
Close: "Weeks 1–3 answered what business do I want to build? Week 4 answers how will I make it work?"
This is the difference between owning a job and building an enterprise.
discussionQuestions: What does your venture gain from platform integration?

slides.title: Your Blueprint Evolves
slides.body:
We're not replacing your Blueprint. We're evolving it.

Blueprint → Platform Integration → Growth Strategy
slides.speakerNotes:
Scene 9 — Have participants open the Blueprint they've been building since Week 1.
Everything they designed over three weeks remains valid.
Today is not about replacing the Blueprint — it evolves.
Platform Integration adds: how the venture uses AIA, which products support solutions, how clients enter, where revenue comes from, how they build a team.
Close: "The entrepreneur with the best idea doesn't always win. The entrepreneur with the best system usually does."

slides.title: Your Blueprint Focus Today
slides.body:
Vision → Business Model → Revenue Model → Growth Strategy

Today's focus: Business Model · Revenue Model
slides.speakerNotes:
Scene 10 — Workshop opens in the Playbook.
Today strengthen only Business Model and Revenue Model.
Business Model: where clients come from, solutions delivered, value created.
Revenue Model: how the business earns income today and five years from now.
Vision gives direction. Business and Revenue Models make it executable.
discussionQuestions: Where do your clients come from?
How will your business earn income five years from now?

slides.title: Your FEC Evolves
slides.body:
Week 3 Foundation: Customers · Problems · Solutions · Value Proposition
↓
Week 4 Integration: Platform Integration · Revenue Engine · Agency Growth Strategy
slides.speakerNotes:
Scene 11 — Open the FEC in the Playbook.
Week 3 foundation stays. Week 4 adds Platform Integration, Revenue Engine, Agency Growth Strategy.
Customers, mission, and value proposition did not change — the business now has a platform to support it.
Workshop: upgrade Blueprint and FEC so they become the operating system for their Financial Advisory Enterprise.
Complete: Blueprint Business Model + Revenue Model, FEC Platform + Revenue Engine + Agency Growth.
discussionQuestions: What changes in your FEC now that you have a platform?
